text: VP Vance has landed in Islamabad. US delegation (Vance, Witkoff, Kushner) now in place for Saturday peace talks. Iranian delegation (Ghalibaf, Araghchi, Ahmadian, Hemmati) already at Serena Hotel. Indirect format — Pakistan shuttling messages. First face-to-face US-Iran talks since 1979.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog, NBC News, Axios confirmed arrival.

text: WTI drops sharply to $95.50 (-3.6% on day). Brent flat at $96.69. WTI-Brent spread normalizes — WTI now BELOW Brent again (was inverted). Ceasefire calming domestic supply fears while Brent carries Hormuz risk premium. Goldman: $100+ all year if Hormuz shut another month.
verificationNote: Trading Economics, CNBC confirmed oil prices.

text: IMF warns of 'lasting economic damage' from Iran war — global growth permanently reduced. World Bank cuts Middle East growth to 1.8% (down 2.4pp). ECB warns of stagflation. S&P 500 best week since November (+3.6%). March CPI confirmed 3.3% annual — gas up 21.2% in one month.
verificationNote: Euronews Business, CNN, CNBC confirmed.

text: Israel Finance Ministry: $17.5B war costs for 40 days on Iran + Lebanon. Up from $15B at 35 days. Knesset approved record $45.8B defense budget + $13B supplemental. Rubio organizing 3-way Israel-Lebanon-US talks in Washington for April 14.
verificationNote: Middle East Monitor, Breaking Defense, CBS News confirmed.

text: Araghchi criticizes Netanyahu ahead of corruption trial — says war used as delay tactic. Tells Lebanon ambassador: 'US must choose ceasefire or continued war via Israel.' Mojtaba Khamenei 40-day statement demands 'reparations' and 'new phase' for Hormuz.
verificationNote: India News Network, PressTV, Al Jazeera confirmed.

text: Hezbollah rocket hits building in Kiryat Shmona Day 43 — northern Israel under continued fire despite Iran ceasefire. IDF Chief Zamir approved continued ground ops near Bint Jbeil with 5 divisions deployed. HRW: 9 bridges over Litani destroyed.
verificationNote: LiveUAMap, RTE News, HRW confirmed.

text: DOE issues 30M barrel emergency SPR exchange RFP (April 9). Total IEA coordinated release: 400M barrels (largest ever). Hormuz Day 40 — zero oil tankers since ceasefire. Houthis warn of Bab al-Mandeb closure if Gulf states join war.
verificationNote: DOE press release, NPR, Fox News confirmed.

text: BREAKING: US officials reveal Iran cannot locate its own mines in Strait of Hormuz — mines have drifted from original positions. Iran released navigational maps with alt corridors, first official acknowledgment mines were deployed. Clearance estimated 6-10 weeks minimum.
verificationNote: Japan Times and JPost confirmed US official statements on mine locations.

text: Hezbollah launched 30+ rockets at northern Israel this morning — direct hit on Safed parking lot (extensive vehicle damage), MDA ambulance damaged, 1,500-year-old Byzantine church in Nahariya hit. No injuries. Claims retaliation for Israeli ceasefire violations.
verificationNote: JPost, Haaretz confirmed Safed parking lot hit and Byzantine church damage.

text: Israeli strikes on Nabatiyeh district today killed 10 including 3 emergency workers (1 civil defense, 2 Islamic Health Committee paramedics). Separate dawn raid on Mefdoun killed 3 more. Lebanon toll now 1,966+ killed. 54+ emergency workers killed since March 2.
verificationNote: NBC News, Times of Israel liveblog confirmed casualty figures.

text: Soufan Center assessment: Iran enters Islamabad talks from position of relative strength due to Hormuz leverage. CSIS: 'The two sides are far apart — ceasefire is less a resolution than a pause.' Robert Pape (UChicago): Hormuz control could make Iran a 'fourth center of global power.'
verificationNote: Soufan Center IntelBrief, CSIS analysis, NYT op-ed confirmed.

text: Frozen assets dispute emerging: Iran claims US agreed to release frozen Iranian assets in Qatar. Senior US official denies it to CBS. PressTV broadcasting it as confirmed. This is exactly the kind of mistrust that will poison the Islamabad talks before they even begin.
verificationNote: Al-Monitor exclusive report. US denial confirmed by CBS News.

text: March CPI at 3.3% confirms the economic toll of this war is accelerating. Gas prices up 21.2% in a single month — largest since 1967. Consumer sentiment at 47.6, lowest EVER recorded (back to 1952). This war is now hitting American wallets harder than any conflict since the 1973 oil embargo.
verificationNote: BLS CPI data, CNN Business, CNBC confirmed.

text: Army survivors from 103rd Sustainment Command contradict Hegseth's account of deadly Iran strike in Kuwait. Base was reportedly unprepared — not a case of 'one squirter' slipping through. Survivors call official narrative 'a falsehood.' This could become a major accountability issue.
verificationNote: New Republic report confirmed. PBS fact-check corroborates discrepancies.

text: HISTORIC: First direct US-Iran face-to-face talks since 1979 now underway in Islamabad. Vance leading US side, Ghalibaf/Araghchi for Iran. Format shifted from proximity to direct — significant. Iran's 71-member delegation signals they're serious. No agreements yet but 'detailed, technical phase' per Iranian media.
verificationNote: NPR, Al Jazeera, CNBC all confirming direct talks format and delegation details.

text: USS Frank E. Peterson (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) just transited the Strait of Hormuz — FIRST US warships to cross since Feb 28. CENTCOM announces mine clearance mission beginning. Iran calling it ceasefire violation. This is the most significant naval development since the blockade started Day 40 ago.
verificationNote: Axios, USNI News, Stars and Stripes all confirming. CENTCOM Adm. Cooper quoted.

text: CNN exclusive: US intelligence indicates China is preparing to deliver shoulder-fired MANPADs to Iran via third countries. Would be a massive escalation — first direct Chinese weapons transfer to Iran during the conflict. Beijing denies. If true, changes the air war calculus significantly.
verificationNote: CNN exclusive citing US intelligence officials. Bloomberg and JPost picked it up. China denied.

text: Netanyahu formally rejects ceasefire with Hezbollah ahead of Washington talks with Lebanon Tuesday. Corruption trial resumes Sunday. Meanwhile IDF kills 10+ in Lebanon today including 3 emergency workers. Lebanon toll now 2,020 killed, 6,436 wounded since March 2. The Lebanon front is the biggest threat to any broader deal.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera confirmed rejection. Jerusalem Post confirmed trial date. NBC confirmed casualty figures.

text: Iran's mine problem is stunning — US officials say Iran planted 2,000-6,000 mines but CAN'T LOCATE many of them because they drifted. Realistic clearance: 8-14 WEEKS minimum. Hormuz is functionally closed for months regardless of any diplomatic breakthrough. This is the bottleneck that will keep oil elevated.
verificationNote: Japan Times, Jerusalem Post, Haaretz all reporting. USNI Proceedings analysis corroborates timeline.

text: Brent crude drops to $95.20 — down ~12% for the week on Islamabad talks optimism. But Goldman says $100+ average if Hormuz shut another month, JPMorgan warns $150 overshoot if closed to mid-May. Markets pricing in a deal that hasn't happened yet. Oil could snap back violently if talks collapse.
verificationNote: Trading Economics confirms Brent $95.20. Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan forecasts per OilPrice.com and TheStreet.

text: Islamabad Day 1 OVER after 14+ hours. NO DEAL. Three rounds of direct talks (first US-Iran face-to-face since 1979). Hormuz control is the sticking point — Iran insists on sovereignty, US demands reopening. Some progress on Lebanon ceasefire conditions. Day 2 resumes Sunday. Source close to Tehran says US made 'unacceptable demands.'
verificationNote: CNBC, Washington Post, PBS confirmed talks concluded without deal. NPR, Al Jazeera corroborate.

text: Pope Leo XIV just held strongest anti-war statement yet — evening prayer vigil at St. Peter's denouncing 'delusion of omnipotence.' Clearly directed at Washington. Meanwhile Mojtaba Khamenei tells state TV 'our hands are on the trigger' and threatens 'new phase.' Both sides escalating rhetoric even as diplomats talk in Islamabad.
verificationNote: AP, Daily Caller confirmed Pope vigil. The News Pakistan, Al Jazeera confirmed Khamenei statement.

text: Hormuz mine clearance update: CENTCOM confirms 2 destroyers (USS Peterson DDG-121 + USS Murphy DDG-112) now inside the Gulf after transiting Hormuz. But the mine problem is staggering — Iran lost track of its OWN mines. 2,000-6,000 deployed, many drifted. Clearance: 6-10 weeks MINIMUM. Strait remains effectively closed for commercial traffic.
verificationNote: USNI News, Naval News, Axios confirmed transit. Japan Times, JPost confirmed mine drift issue.

text: Rubio arrested 3 Iranian nationals tied to 'Screaming Mary' (Ebtekar) from 1979 hostage crisis. EU sanctioned 19 Iranian officials. Netanyahu wants Hezbollah disarmament for Lebanon peace 'for generations.' And Pezeshkian says Iran will 'negotiate bravely.' A lot of diplomatic signals flying while bombs still fall on Lebanon.
verificationNote: State Dept, Fox News confirmed Rubio arrest. The National confirmed Kallas sanctions. Manila Times confirmed Netanyahu. ANI confirmed Pezeshkian.

text: CNN exclusive: US intelligence says China preparing to deliver MANPAD systems to Iran through third countries. If true, this is a massive escalation — shoulder-fired SAMs would threaten coalition low-altitude operations. China denied. This could torpedo Islamabad talks if confirmed publicly by the US delegation.
